title: Incident auto-creation from observations
prompt:
Implement incident auto-creation that converts nullbot observations/findings into JIRA issues or GitHub issues.

The JIRA client already exists in internal/jira/ with Creator interface: CreateIssue(ctx, input) (*Issue, error). The finding types are in internal/observe/ — findings have Type, Severity, Detail, Hash fields.

Create internal/incident/creator.go:
- IncidentCreator struct with Config (JIRAConfig or GitHubConfig), dedup store
- CreateFromFinding(ctx, finding) (*Incident, error) — creates issue from finding
- CreateFromFindings(ctx, findings []Finding) ([]*Incident, error) — batch creation
- Deduplication: check finding hash against SQLite store, skip if open incident exists
- Incident struct: ID, Source (jira/github), Key, URL, FindingHash, CreatedAt

Create internal/incident/jira_backend.go:
- Implements Backend interface using internal/jira.Creator
- Maps finding severity to JIRA priority (critical→Highest, high→High, medium→Medium, low→Low)
- Issue title: '[chainwatch] {finding.Type}: {truncated detail}'
- Issue body: finding details, severity, recommended action, chainwatch trace link
- Labels: 'chainwatch', 'auto-created', severity label

Create internal/incident/github_backend.go:
- Implements Backend interface using GitHub Issues API (gh CLI or direct HTTP)
- Maps severity to labels
- Issue title and body similar to JIRA

Create internal/incident/store.go:
- SQLite store for incident dedup (incident_id, finding_hash, source, key, status, created_at)
- Open(path) (*Store, error)
- FindByHash(hash string) (*Incident, error)
- Save(incident *Incident) error
- Close() error

Create internal/incident/creator_test.go:
- Test dedup: same finding hash → skip
- Test batch creation: 3 findings → 3 incidents
- Test severity mapping
- Use mock backend (no real JIRA/GitHub calls)

Verification: go test -race ./internal/incident/... -v

IMPORTANT: After completing all changes, you MUST commit your work. Run `git add` for changed files and `git commit` with a conventional commit message (e.g. feat:, fix:, refactor:). A task is NOT complete until changes are committed.
